title: physaria carinata subsp. paysonii
intro: Physaria carinata subsp. paysonii, also known as Payson bladderpod, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Brassicaceae family. It is a rare and endangered plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, and has been used to treat conditions like arthritis and wounds. Physaria carinata subsp. paysonii can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and extracts, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses.
